calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-19-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Wayne Smith for Executor. James Frank in Propria Persona.

Mr. Smith stated that he exchanged documents with Mr. Gale, that he thought they had a settlement, that Mr. Gale was dismissed by Mr. Frank and that Mr. Frank had previously signed at least four waivers. Mr. Frank stated that he signed the waivers because the Estate owed him money, that there was no comprehensible accounting ever produced and that he was advised not to sign this waiver. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-23-2010 Continued To Mar-16-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Wayne Smith for Dorothy Trovato (Executor/Petitioner). Barton Gale for and with James Frank (Beneficiary).

Mr. Gale stated that his Client signed an earlier waiver as he believed there would be additional costs if the Executor was to account and that he signed the release as there was litigation within his Client and the Executor. Mr. Smith stated that it was a mutual release and settlement, that it had nothing to do with fraud, that Mr. Frank went over the documents at the Sister's house, that the documents were signed went along with distributions. At the suggestion of the Court, the Parties agreed to exchange information and try to work it out. The matter was continued to March 16, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Off Calendar Sep-03-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Denenberg for James Frank (Petitioner).

Mr. Denenberg stated that he spoke with Mr. Browne (counsel for Velocity Capital) and that he agreed to drop his demand for any portion of James Frank's share. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR#5162. Clerk: J. Alameda/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Wong.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Aug-18-2008 Continued To Sep-03-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Deneberg for James Frank and Bert Frank. John Brown for Velocity Capital LLC. Joyce Hee from the Attorney General's office for the Franchise Tax Board.

The Parties stipulated to continuing James Frank's petition to September 3, 2008.

The Parties presented a stipulation and order for distribution of share of estate for Bert Joseph Frank for the Court's signature which was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Guitron.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-30-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Browne for Velocity Capital, LLC. James Frank in Pro Per. Wayne Smith for Executor. Dan Deneberg for Burt Frank.

The petition for modification of the preliminary distribution was granted as there were no objections. Mr. Browne cited Probate Code 11604 and stated that Velocity Capital received interest for Littlewood Financial Group, that they got a $100,000.00 note, that Littlewood had the client's assigned interest in the estate, that Burt signed the agreement and that they had nothing to do with setting the rates and fees. Mr. Denenberg stated that the Franchise Tax Board needed to state thier position and that the Court should not rule. The Court suggested filing a petition for instructions and giving notice to all interested parties. The remaining issues were taken under submission. Counsel is to prepare orders after hearings and circulate them.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Aguerre.
